content.full_text:
Farn Pourrcal PnacrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 9581+2329
(916) 322-56ffi . Fax (916) 322-0886
October 8,20A4
Thomas W. Hiltachk
Bell, McAndrews & Hiltachk,LLP
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 801
Sacrarrrento, CA95814
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-204
Dear Mr. Hiltachk:
This letter is to notiff you that we have withdrawn the above-captioned request
for advice on behalf of Oakland Harbor Partners, LLC and Signature Properties regarding
section 84308 of the Political Reform Act.l This is yoursecond request for advicelo" ttii
topic. The first question, which was almost identical to this inquiry, was withdrawn on
September 15,2004, because it was determined to be a request for third party advice and
included facts relating to past conduct ofthe pffiy. We still cannot provide you with
advice as this request also involves the duties of third parties.
The duties of a "participant" under section 84308 concern the prohibition on the
making of contributions either during or for the threemonths following a final decision.
You have stated that the reason for your inquiry is for a determination of whether a
contribution under section 84308 applies to state constitutional officers, specifically Lt.
Governor Cruz Bustamante and Csntroller Steve Westly, and whethe,r sueh a conkibution
should be made by a "participant" you represent. Although we understand that this
knowledge would assist your clie,nt in deciding whether or not to make a conhibution, the
advice would be a determination of the rights and responsibilities of Lt. Governor
Bustamante or Controller Westly under the Act, and not the "participant" you represent.
Should either Lt. Governor Cruz Bustamante or Controller Steve Westly authorize you to
request advice on his behalf, then we would be happy to provide you with the advice you
seek. Pursuant to regulation 18329(bXSXBXcopy enclosed), written advice or assistance
is not provided in response to unauthorized third party requests.
I Government Code sections 8 1000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


FileNo. W-04-204
Page No. 2
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Galena West
Counsel, l* gal Division
Gwje
Enclosure
I:\Adviceltrs\W-04-2M

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a contribution under Political Reform Act section 84308 applies to state constitutional officers (Lt. Governor Cruz Bustamante and Controller Steve Westly), and whether such a contribution should be made by a represented participant.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because it constitutes an unauthorized third-party request seeking to determine the rights and responsibilities of the state constitutional officers rather than the requesting participant, and such advice cannot be provided without authorization from the affected officers themselves.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter, not a standard advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections exist. The document explains the reason for withdrawal and references a prior identical withdrawn request from September 15, 2004. Legal question and conclusion are synthesized from the body text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a contribution under Political Reform Act section 84308 applies to state constitutional officers (Lt. Governor Cruz Bustamante and Controller Steve Westly), and whether such a contribution should be made by a represented participant.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because it constitutes an unauthorized third-party request seeking to determine the rights and responsibilities of the state constitutional officers rather than the requesting participant, and such advice cannot be provided without authorization from the affected officers themselves.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew an advice request from an attorney regarding Political Reform Act section 84308 contribution restrictions, determining it was an impermissible third-party request that would require determining the duties of state constitutional officers rather than the requesting participant.
